title: chronopappus lanatus
intro: Chronopappus lanatus, also known as the Woolly Chronopappus, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family and is native to the high-altitude mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Chronopappus lanatus include its ability to soothe sore throats and relieve congestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds and flu. Chronopappus lanatus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be taken orally or used topically to alleviate respiratory symptoms.
